concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to store graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.

concepts.name: Stack Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A mechanism to keep track of nodes that have already been explored during traversal.

concepts.name: Traversal Order
concepts.short_definition: The sequence in which nodes are visited during a graph traversal.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Subgraphs where any two vertices are connected to each other by paths.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains a cycle.

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: A linear ordering of vertices such that for every directed edge uv from vertex u to vertex v, u comes before v in the ordering.

concepts.name: Applications of DFS
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems that can be solved using Depth-First Search.
